spec_match.rationale: Mirrors the attribute-name side of the relaxation: setAttribute with colon/unicode/leading-dash names, parity-checked against the parser's innerHTML path.

interactive.rationale: Tag + attr + value text inputs plus eight quick-pick pills drive a live try/catch on both setAttribute and the parser path, with red/green result cards and live outerHTML preview.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises 'type any name, watch the parser and setAttribute finally agree' — the page delivers that side-by-side parity check.

edge_cases.rationale: Pills include the canonical fail cases (`100`, `foo!`) and unicode/colon successes, but never demonstrate the duplicate-attribute behaviour, empty-string attribute name, or what setAttributeNS does with the same inputs — all worth a row.

references.rationale: See-also lists https://chromestatus.com/feature/6278918763708416 and the DOM namespaces spec.

design_system.rationale: All colours from CSS variables; ok/bad use --accent-emerald / --accent-rose with default --text-black, AA-clean.

openQuestions.title: Add a setAttributeNS row
openQuestions.detail: Visitors who hit this page from the SVG/MathML round-trip will wonder whether the namespaced form (`setAttributeNS('http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink', 'xlink:href', '#x')`) is affected by the same relaxation. Adding a third result column for the NS variant would close the loop in one panel rather than sending them to another concept.

openQuestions.title: Show the duplicate-attribute behaviour
openQuestions.detail: setAttribute(name, value) is case-insensitive on HTML elements but case-sensitive on non-HTML. Add a 'set twice with different cases' probe so the spec's case rules show up explicitly rather than being inferred from the outerHTML lowercasing.

openQuestions.title: Test the still-rejected attribute names
openQuestions.detail: The empty string, names containing `=`, `/`, `>`, and U+0020 should still throw InvalidCharacterError. A 'still-fails' pill row would teach the boundary the spec preserves.

summary: Strong attribute-side parity demo. Two follow-ons would round it out: a setAttributeNS row (for the SVG/xlink crossover) and explicit still-rejected pills so the relaxed boundary is visible.
